Description.Name: Point of No Return
Description.Details: The Argo drops out of its final jump into the Fröislandis system, deep in the [[DM.BaseDescriptionDefs[LoreAntiSpinward],anti-spinward]] sector of the [[DM.BaseDescriptionDefs[LoreDeepPeriphery],Deep Periphery]] past the Cygnus Loop. Sensors immediately begin sweeping the frozen planet below. Marcus Hammerick's face appears on the viewscreen before anyone can speak. His expression is grim. "Commander {COMMANDER.Callsign}, the tactical situation is more complex than we anticipated. Joanna has fortified the facility with multiple defensive layers. You'll need to execute a two-phase assault with no pause between operations." "Phase One is breaching the outer perimeter defenses. Joanna has deployed her most fanatical [[DM.Factions[faction_RemembranceCell],Remembrance Cell]] warriors to guard the facility approaches. Elite guards who know the terrain and are prepared to die protecting what they believe is their people's salvation. You must destroy these forces completely to gain access to the inner facility." "Phase Two begins the moment the outer perimeter falls. You will immediately assault the Armageddon production facility itself where the prototype is being held. There is no time to regroup or repair. The moment Joanna realizes her outer defenses have failed, she will deploy the Armageddon mech and throw every remaining warrior at you." "This is a continuous operation. Breach the perimeter, then immediately push into the facility without stopping. If you give Joanna time to prepare, she may escape with the prototype or technical data. You cannot allow that." Sumire pulls up the tactical data. "Frozen world with significant sensor interference. Limited visibility from atmospheric conditions and constant snowfall. And we're conducting two consecutive assaults with no recovery time between them." Darius looks grim. "Boss, this is going to push us past our limits. Two full engagements back to back in terrible conditions against fanatics who believe they're fighting for their survival." Farah reviews the data. "We have Dr. Tilsaye's complete technical specifications. We know the facility layout, the defensive systems, the weapon capabilities. That intelligence advantage matters, but we'll be exhausted by the time we reach the inner facility." Yang checks ammunition reserves. "We'll be running on fumes by the second assault. Better make the first one count." Marcus's expression becomes even more serious, almost haunted. "Commander, now that we are at the point of no return, I need you to understand something. I have acquired a two hundred gigaton nuclear warhead. The only delivery method for a warhead of this magnitude is ramming a DropShip directly into the surface at terminal velocity. I have a vessel prepared for that purpose." Yang's face goes pale. "Two hundred gigaton." Marcus nods with a guilty expression."If it detonates, the blast will consume everything within hundreds of kilometers. The thermal pulse will be visible from orbit. Fröislandis VII's ice caps will flash-boil. The planetary surface will be permanently scarred." "If the conventional assault fails and there is any risk of Joanna escaping with the prototype technology or technical data, I will order that DropShip to ram the facility and detonate on impact." "Everyone at Fröislandis VII dies in that scenario. Myself and all my forces. Your company. Every Remembrance Cell fanatic. Even the Argo if it's anywhere near the surface point of impact. The small research outpost on the planet with innocent researchers. Everything. The planet itself will bear the scars for centuries." "The Armageddon weapon's technology cannot be allowed to survive if we cannot secure it for destruction. I am willing to sacrifice everyone there, including myself, to ensure this weapon does not reach deployment status." "You need to win, Commander. You need to destroy that weapon before I have to make that choice. Because if you fail, I will detonate that warhead. I will kill everyone on that planet, including you and your crew. Win at any cost. There is no other option. Good luck and godspeed, Commander. If this fails, there won't be anyone left to bury the dead." The transmission ends abruptly. Silence fills the command deck. No one moves. No one speaks.

Description.Name: [Look at the others in disbelief]
Description.Details: Farah is the first to break the silence, her voice shaking. "Two hundred gigaton. That's from the Old Kentucky project. The [[DM.Factions[faction_FreeWorldsLeague],Free Worlds League]] deployed one in 2787 and it permanently scarred the planet. They abandoned the entire program because the weapon was too destructive to control. How did Marcus acquire one?" Darius barely manages to speak. "Does it matter how? Two hundred gigaton. That's more of a massive geological event than a weapon." Yang's voice shakes. "He has a weapon that can scar an entire planet. If he detonates that thing, we don't just die. We get atomized along with everything else, nothing would remain but superheated dust and plasma." Sumire's hands are shaking as she pulls up a basic simulation based on historical data. "He told us the consequences while we were still on the call. He wanted to make sure we understood he'd already made the decision." Farah looks stricken. "Marcus has a weapon that was used once in human history and considered so horrifying they never used it again. He's willing to use it on us if we fail. On an planet inhabited by innocent civilian researchers." Darius looks at you, his expression showing barely controlled terror. "Commander, we're already here. We're already committed. Two engagements back to back, no recovery time, no reinforcements, no extraction option. If we fail either of the assaults, we don't get a second chance, Marcus makes that choice for all of us. There's no backing out now." Yang's voice is tight with fury and fear. "So we fight through two consecutive assaults, destroy a superheavy prototype, and make absolutely certain we win. Because if we don't, Marcus erases us and everything else on this planet." Sumire forces herself to breathe carefully. "Win both, or none of us leave this planet. That's the arithmetic. There is no middle ground, no reinforcements available, no withdrawal, no rescue. No margin for error. And we can't leave. We're already here. The weapon is here. Marcus is here with a doomsday device." Farah's voice is barely steady. "Then we win. We breach the perimeter and we destroy the weapon without stopping. We get off this planet before Marcus has to detonate that warhead and kill everyone on the surface." The crew looks to you. They're terrified, but they're committed. They are ready for one final operation. [A new contract is available in the Command Center]
